claim: Management should establish an information security program approved by the board.
source_clauses: Management should establish an information security program approved by the board.
The program addresses risk identification, measurement, monitoring, and control.

claim: Independent audit must validate the effectiveness of internal controls.
source_clauses: Independent audit must validate the effectiveness of internal controls.
Audit findings are reported to the audit committee.

claim: Third-party service providers are subject to ongoing due diligence.
source_clauses: Third-party service providers are subject to ongoing due diligence.
Concentration risk should be considered when selecting providers.

claim: Business continuity plans must be tested at least annually.
source_clauses: Business continuity plans must be tested at least annually.
Disaster recovery exercises validate recovery time objectives.

claim: Customer authentication should employ multifactor methods for high-risk transactions.
source_clauses: Customer authentication should employ multifactor methods for high-risk transactions.
Layered security controls reduce the impact of single-control failures.

claim: Senior leadership establishes a board-sanctioned cybersecurity framework.
source_clauses: Management should establish an information security program approved by the board.
Cybersecurity governance falls under board-level oversight.

claim: Internal control effectiveness is verified by an independent assessor.
source_clauses: Independent audit must validate the effectiveness of internal controls.
Auditor independence preserves objectivity.

claim: Vendor relationships require continuous risk reassessment.
source_clauses: Third-party service providers are subject to ongoing due diligence.
Annual reviews are the minimum cadence for material vendors.

claim: Recovery plans undergo yearly exercise validation.
source_clauses: Business continuity plans must be tested at least annually.
Tabletop exercises and full-failover tests serve different validation purposes.

claim: High-value transactions trigger stronger identity verification.
source_clauses: Customer authentication should employ multifactor methods for high-risk transactions.
Risk-based authentication adapts to transaction characteristics.

claim: Encryption is required when financial data crosses network boundaries.
source_clauses: Sensitive financial data must be encrypted in transit.
TLS 1.2 or higher is the minimum acceptable protocol version.

claim: Cyber events demand prompt notification to relevant regulators.
source_clauses: Material cybersecurity incidents must be reported to regulators within 36 hours.
Notification timelines are codified in CIRCIA and similar regulations.

claim: Management may delegate the entire security program to outside counsel.
source_clauses: Management should establish an information security program approved by the board.
Outside counsel advises on regulatory matters but does not own the program.

claim: Self-audit is sufficient to validate internal controls.
source_clauses: Independent audit must validate the effectiveness of internal controls.
Internal audit reports to the audit committee, not management.

claim: Vendor due diligence is completed once at contract signing only.
source_clauses: Third-party service providers are subject to ongoing due diligence.
Initial due diligence is necessary but not sufficient.

claim: Business continuity plans should remain confidential and untested.
source_clauses: Business continuity plans must be tested at least annually.
Confidentiality controls protect plan distribution but do not prevent testing.

claim: Single-factor authentication is acceptable for wire transfers above $1M.
source_clauses: Customer authentication should employ multifactor methods for high-risk transactions.
Wire transfers are inherently high-risk transactions.

claim: Financial data may be transmitted in cleartext within the corporate network.
source_clauses: Sensitive financial data must be encrypted in transit.
Internal network segments do not exempt data from encryption requirements.

claim: Cyber incidents may be reported when convenient at quarter-end.
source_clauses: Material cybersecurity incidents must be reported to regulators within 36 hours.
Delayed reporting carries enforcement risk.

claim: FFIEC publishes a quarterly art catalog featuring board members.
source_clauses: Management should establish an information security program approved by the board.
Independent audit must validate the effectiveness of internal controls.

claim: Customer-facing tellers must hum the national anthem at shift change.
source_clauses: Customer authentication should employ multifactor methods for high-risk transactions.
Sensitive financial data must be encrypted in transit.

claim: All vendor contracts include a clause permitting ritual cake-cutting.
source_clauses: Third-party service providers are subject to ongoing due diligence.
Material cybersecurity incidents must be reported to regulators within 36 hours.

claim: Regulatory examinations are scheduled by lottery draw.
source_clauses: Independent audit must validate the effectiveness of internal controls.
Business continuity plans must be tested at least annually.

claim: Password complexity rules align with the lunar calendar.
source_clauses: Customer authentication should employ multifactor methods for high-risk transactions.
Sensitive financial data must be encrypted in transit.
